mappings.rationaleAI: Government's exercise of binding authority through legislative, executive, and judicial branches encompasses the ways political power is exercised (A) and government's role in production and consumption (B).
Democracy's popular sovereignty and electoral processes address how political power is exercised (A) and peaceful changes of government by electoral process (C.1).

Dictatorship's exercise of absolute power through elite cooptation and opposition repression addresses how political power is exercised (A), while coup theory connects to methods of changing the form of government (C).
Police as state bodies empowered to enforce law and maintain public order address internal security functions (A.1) and law enforcement and corrective functions of government (A.5).

The military as a state-organized armed force for defense and deterrence addresses internal and external security functions (A.1) and the conduct of foreign relations with other sovereign states (A.2).
Government-funded social insurance and social assistance addressing basic needs exemplify enterprising functions of government (A.6) and government's role in production and consumption (B).
Trade unions negotiating wages and working conditions through collective bargaining connect to regulatory functions of government in establishing and enforcing labor standards (A.4).
Human rights frameworks enforced through UN treaty bodies, international courts, and national legislation address regulatory functions of government in establishing and enforcing standards (A.4).

Crime as unlawful acts punishable by state authority, with criminal justice encompassing prevention, law enforcement, sentencing, and rehabilitation, directly addresses law enforcement and corrective functions of government (A.5).
Imperialism as extension of power over foreign territories through military and economic coercion addresses external security functions (A.1) and the conduct of foreign relations (A.2).
Ideology as a system of beliefs legitimizing social arrangements and reproducing power relations broadly connects to the ways in which political power is exercised (A).
Monarchy's hereditary succession and constitutional versus absolute forms address methods of changing and maintaining the form of government (C).
Anarchism's advocacy for abolition of the state connects to methods of changing the form of government (C) through revolutionary transformation.
Socialism's planned and state-directed economic models directly address government's role in production and consumption (B).
Liberty as lawful autonomy constrained by rule of law and contested across political ideologies addresses regulatory functions of government in balancing freedom and order (A.4).
Social and political power operating through coercion, institutional structure, and hegemony directly addresses the ways in which political power is exercised (A).
The economy as the domain of production, distribution, and consumption shaped by government policy directly addresses government's role in production and consumption (B).
Poverty reduction strategies including state transfers and income grants connect to enterprising functions of government (A.6) and government's role in production and consumption (B).
Diplomacy as managing international relations through negotiation addresses the conduct of foreign relations (A.2) and supervisory functions through mediation and conflict resolution (A.3).
The state exercising sovereign authority and its theories of function (social contract, Marxist, anarchist) encompass the ways political power is exercised (A).

Lincoln's leadership through the Civil War addresses internal security functions of government (A.1), while the Emancipation Proclamation and constitutional amendments exemplify methods of changing government through constitutional mandate (C).
